NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. To You I will cry, O LORD my Rock: Do not be silent to me, Lest, if You are silent to me, I become like those who go down to the pit.
NKJV.2: Hear the voice of my supplications When I cry to You,  When I lift up my hands toward Your holy sanctuary.
NKJV.3: Do not take me away with the wicked And with the workers of iniquity, Who speak peace to their neighbors, But evil is in their hearts.
NKJV.4: Give them according to their deeds, And according to the wickedness of their endeavors; Give them according to the work of their hands; Render to them what they deserve.
NKJV.5: Because they do not regard the works of the LORD, Nor the operation of His hands, He shall destroy them And not build them up.
NKJV.6: Blessed be the LORD, Because He has heard the voice of my supplications!
NKJV.7: The LORD is my strength and my shield; My heart trusted in Him, and I am helped; Therefore my heart greatly rejoices, And with my song I will praise Him.
NKJV.8: The LORD is their strength, And He is the saving refuge of His anointed.
NKJV.9: Save Your people, And bless Your inheritance; Shepherd them also,  And bear them up forever.

KJV.3: Draw me not away<S>4900</S> with the wicked<S>7563</S>, and with the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S>, which speak<S>1696</S> peace<S>7965</S> to their neighbours<S>7453</S>, but mischief<S>7451</S> is in their hearts<S>3824</S>.
KJV.4: Give<S>5414</S> them according to their deeds<S>6467</S>, and according to the wickedness<S>7455</S> of their endeavours<S>4611</S>: give<S>5414</S> them after the work<S>4639</S> of their hands<S>3027</S>; render<S>7725</S> to them their desert<S>1576</S>.
KJV.5: Because they regard<S>995</S> not the works<S>6468</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, nor the operation<S>4639</S> of his hands<S>3027</S>, he shall destroy<S>2040</S> them, and not build them up<S>1129</S>.

ESV.1: To you, O LORD, I call; my rock, be not deaf to me, lest, if you be silent to me, I become like those who go down to the pit.
ESV.2: Hear the voice of my pleas for mercy, when I cry to you for help, when I lift up my hands toward your most holy sanctuary.
ESV.3: Do not drag me off with the wicked, with the workers of evil, who speak peace with their neighbors while evil is in their hearts.
ESV.4: Give to them according to their work and according to the evil of their deeds; give to them according to the work of their hands; render them their due reward.
ESV.5: Because they do not regard the works of the LORD or the work of his hands, he will tear them down and build them up no more.
ESV.6: Blessed be the LORD! For he has heard the voice of my pleas for mercy.
ESV.7: The LORD is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusts, and I am helped; my heart exults, and with my song I give thanks to him.
ESV.8: The LORD is the strength of his people;  he is the saving refuge of his anointed.
ESV.9: Oh, save your people and bless your heritage! Be their shepherd and carry them forever.

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. To You, O LORD, I call; My rock, do not be deaf to me, For if You are silent to me, I will become like those who go down to the pit.
NASB.2: Hear the voice of my supplications when I cry to You for help, When I lift up my hands toward Your holy sanctuary.
NASB.3: Do not drag me away with the wicked And with those who work iniquity, Who speak peace with their neighbors, While evil is in their hearts.
NASB.4: Requite them according to their work and according to the evil of their practices; Requite them according to the deeds of their hands; Repay them their recompense.
NASB.5: Because they do not regard the works of the LORD Nor the deeds of His hands, He will tear them down and not build them up.
NASB.6: Blessed be the LORD, Because He has heard the voice of my supplication.
NASB.7: The LORD is my strength and my shield; My heart trusts in Him, and I am helped; Therefore my heart exults, And with my song I shall thank Him.
NASB.8: The LORD is their strength, And He is a saving defense to His anointed.
NASB.9: Save Your people and bless Your inheritance; Be their shepherd also, and carry them forever.

NLT.1:
I pray to you, O LORD, my rock.
Do not turn a deaf ear to me.
For if you are silent,
I might as well give up and die.
NLT.2:
Listen to my prayer for mercy
as I cry out to you for help,
as I lift my hands toward your holy sanctuary.
NLT.3:
Do not drag me away with the wicked —
with those who do evil —
those who speak friendly words to their neighbors
while planning evil in their hearts.
NLT.4:
Give them the punishment they so richly deserve!
Measure it out in proportion to their wickedness.
Pay them back for all their evil deeds!
Give them a taste of what they have done to others.
NLT.5:
They care nothing for what the LORD has done
or for what his hands have made.
So he will tear them down,
and they will never be rebuilt!
NLT.6:
Praise the LORD!
For he has heard my cry for mercy.
NLT.7:
The LORD is my strength and shield.
I trust him with all my heart.
He helps me, and my heart is filled with joy.
I burst out in songs of thanksgiving.
NLT.8:
The LORD gives his people strength.
He is a safe fortress for his anointed king.
NLT.9:
Save your people!
Bless Israel, your special possession.
Lead them like a shepherd,
and carry them in your arms forever.

WEB.1: By David. To you, Yahweh, I call. My rock, don’t be deaf to me; lest, if you are silent to me, I would become like those who go down into the pit.
WEB.2: Hear the voice of my petitions, when I cry to you, when I lift up my hands toward your Most Holy Place.
WEB.3: Don’t draw me away with the wicked, with the workers of iniquity who speak peace with their neighbors, but mischief is in their hearts.
WEB.4: Give them according to their work, and according to the wickedness of their doings. Give them according to the operation of their hands. Bring back on them what they deserve.
WEB.5: Because they don’t respect the works of Yahweh, nor the operation of his hands, he will break them down and not build them up.
WEB.6: Blessed be Yahweh, because he has heard the voice of my petitions.
WEB.7: Yahweh is my strength and my shield. My heart has trusted in him, and I am helped. Therefore my heart greatly rejoices. With my song I will thank him.
WEB.8: Yahweh is their strength. He is a stronghold of salvation to his anointed.
WEB.9: Save your people, and bless your inheritance. Be their shepherd also, and bear them up forever.

CSB17.1:
Of David. [1] LORD, I call to you;
my rock, do not be deaf to me. ⓕ
If you remain silent to me,
I will be like those going down to the Pit. ⓖ
CSB17.2:
Listen to the sound of my pleading
when I cry to you for help,
when I lift up my hands
toward your holy sanctuary. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
Do not drag me away with the wicked,
with the evildoers,
who speak in friendly ways with their neighbors
while malice is in their hearts. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
Repay them according to what they have done —
according to the evil of their deeds.
Repay them according to the work of their hands;
give them back what they deserve. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
Because they do not consider
what the LORD has done
or the work of his hands,
he will tear them down and not rebuild them. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
Blessed be the LORD,
for he has heard the sound of my pleading. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
The LORD is my strength and my shield;
my heart trusts in him, and I am helped. ⓜ
Therefore my heart celebrates,
and I give thanks to him with my song. ⓝ
CSB17.8:
The LORD is the strength of his people; [1]
he is a stronghold of salvation for his anointed. ⓐ
CSB17.9:
Save your people, bless your possession,
shepherd them, and carry them forever. ⓑ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. To you I call, O LORD,
My rock, do not be deaf to me,
For if You are silent to me,
I will become like those who go down to the pit (grave).
AMP.2:
Hear the voice of my supplication (specific requests, humble entreaties) as I cry to You for help,
As I lift up my hands and heart toward Your innermost sanctuary (Holy of Holies).
AMP.3:
Do not drag me away with the wicked
And with those who do evil,
Who speak peace with their neighbors,
While malice and mischief are in their hearts.
AMP.4:
Repay them according to their work and according to the evil of their practices;
Repay them according to the deeds of their hands;
Repay them what they deserve.
AMP.5:
Because they have no regard for the works of the LORD
Nor the deeds of His hands,
He will tear them down and not rebuild them.
AMP.6:
Blessed be the LORD,
Because He has heard the voice of my supplication.
AMP.7:
The LORD is my strength and my [impenetrable] shield;
My heart trusts [with unwavering confidence] in Him, and I am helped;
Therefore my heart greatly rejoices,
And with my song I shall thank Him and praise Him.
AMP.8:
The LORD is their [unyielding] strength,
And He is the fortress of salvation to His anointed.
AMP.9:
Save Your people and bless Your inheritance;
Be their shepherd also, and carry them forever.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Don't turn a deaf ear
when I call you, God.
If all I get from you is
deafening silence,
I'd be better off
in the Black Hole.
MSG.2:
I'm letting you know what I need,
calling out for help
And lifting my arms
toward your inner sanctum.
MSG.3:
Don't shove me into
the same jail cell with those crooks,
With those who are
full-time employees of evil.
MSG.4:
They talk a good line of "peace,"
then moonlight for the Devil.
Pay them back for what they've done,
for how bad they've been.
Pay them back for their long hours
in the Devil's workshop;
Then cap it with a huge bonus.
MSG.5:
Because they have no idea how God works
or what he is up to,
God will smash them to smithereens
and walk away from the ruins.
MSG.6:
Blessed be God—
he heard me praying.
MSG.7:
He proved he's on my side;
I've thrown my lot in with him.
Now I'm jumping for joy,
and shouting and singing my thanks to him.
MSG.8:
God is all strength for his people,
ample refuge for his chosen leader;
MSG.9:
Save your people
and bless your heritage.
Care for them;
carry them like a good shepherd.

NIV.1:
Psalm 28
Of David.
To you I call, O Lord my Rock;
do not turn a deaf ear to me.
For if you remain silent,
I will be like those who have gone down to the pit.
NIV.2:
Hear my cry for mercy
as I call to you for help,
as I lift up my hands
toward your Most Holy Place.
NIV.3:
Do not drag me away with the wicked,
with those who do evil,
who speak cordially with their neighbors
but harbor malice in their hearts.
NIV.4:
Repay them for their deeds
and for their evil work;
repay them for what their hands have done
and bring back upon them what they deserve.
NIV.5:
Since they show no regard for the works of the Lord
and what his hands have done,
he will tear them down
and never build them up again.
NIV.6:
Praise be to the Lord,
for he has heard my cry for mercy.
NIV.7:
The Lord is my strength and my shield;
my heart trusts in him, and I am helped.
My heart leaps for joy
and I will give thanks to him in song.
NIV.8:
The Lord is the strength of his people,
a fortress of salvation for his anointed one.
NIV.9:
Save your people and bless your inheritance;
be their shepherd and carry them forever.

NRSVCE.1:
To you, O LORD, I call;
my rock, do not refuse to hear me,
for if you are silent to me,
I shall be like those who go down to the Pit.
NRSVCE.2:
Hear the voice of my supplication,
as I cry to you for help,
as I lift up my hands
toward your most holy sanctuary.
NRSVCE.3:
Do not drag me away with the wicked,
with those who are workers of evil,
who speak peace with their neighbors,
while mischief is in their hearts.
NRSVCE.4:
Repay them according to their work,
and according to the evil of their deeds;
repay them according to the work of their hands;
render them their due reward.
NRSVCE.5:
Because they do not regard the works of the LORD,
or the work of his hands,
he will break them down and build them up no more.
NRSVCE.6:
Blessed be the LORD,
for he has heard the sound of my pleadings.
NRSVCE.7:
The LORD is my strength and my shield;
in him my heart trusts;
so I am helped, and my heart exults,
and with my song I give thanks to him.
NRSVCE.8:
The LORD is the strength of his people;
he is the saving refuge of his anointed.
NRSVCE.9:
O save your people, and bless your heritage;
be their shepherd, and carry them forever.

NET.1: To you, O LORD, I cry out! My protector, do not ignore me! If you do not respond to me, I will join those who are descending into the grave.
NET.2: Hear my plea for mercy when I cry out to you for help, when I lift my hands toward your holy temple!
NET.3: Do not drag me away with evil men, with those who behave wickedly, who talk so friendly to their neighbors, while they plan to harm them!
NET.4: Pay them back for their evil deeds! Pay them back for what they do! Punish them!
NET.5: For they do not understand the LORD’s actions, or the way he carries out justice. The LORD will permanently demolish them.
NET.6: The LORD deserves praise, for he has heard my plea for mercy!
NET.7: The LORD strengthens and protects me; I trust in him with all my heart. I am rescued and my heart is full of joy; I will sing to him in gratitude.
NET.8: The LORD strengthens his people; he protects and delivers his chosen king.
NET.9: Deliver your people! Empower the nation that belongs to you! Care for them like a shepherd and carry them in your arms at all times!
